content.problemStatement:
Alice is going for a drive. During her drive, she reached a maximum speed of SSS km/hr.

As per the rules of the government, the speed of the vehicle must **not exceed** 404040 km/hr, otherwise the person will be fined.

You need to tell whether Alice will be fined or not.


Input Format

The only line of input will contain a single integer SSS - denoting the maximum speed Alice reached while driving.


Output Format
Print `YES` if Alice will be fined , otherwise print `NO`.


You may print each character of the string in uppercase or lowercase (for example, the strings `YES`, `yEs`, `yes`, and `yeS` will all be treated as identical).
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer S (1 ≤ S ≤ 50) – the maximum speed reached by Alice in that test case.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print "YES" if S > 40 (Alice will be fined), otherwise print "NO". Each answer should be on its own line.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Alice's speed is exactly the limit (40 km/hr), so she is not fined.
Alice's speed exceeds the limit, therefore she will be fined.
First two speeds are ≤40 → NO, third speed is >40 → YES.

solution.approach: For each test case read the speed S and compare it with the legal limit 40 km/hr. If S is greater than 40 output YES, else output NO. This is O(1) per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work for each of the T test cases.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few integer variables are used.
